question.text: Under SEBI PIT Regulations 2015, an "immediate relative" includes all of the following EXCEPT:

answer.text: A cousin who lives independently and does not consult on trading decisions
A financially dependent parent of the person
A child of the spouse who consults on trading decisions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under PIT Regulations, "immediate relative" means the spouse, and parents, siblings, or children of the person or spouse — but only if they are financially dependent on the person OR consult the person on trading decisions. A cousin is not included in this definition regardless of financial dependence. **The Trap:** Candidates may think any close family member qualifies. The definition is restrictive — it covers only spouse, parents, siblings, and children (of either the person or spouse), plus the financial dependence or consultation condition. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests the precise definition of "immediate relative" as it determines who is deemed a connected person under PIT Regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When advising clients on insider trading compliance, map out the exact family members covered — extending prohibitions to cousins, uncles, or in-laws beyond the defined scope is unnecessary.
